## [00:00 - 03:01]

Now I want to show you how I look at video engagement. So we've been through different kinds of KPIs that give you lots of insight as to what's going on with the data. But this example is how I look at video engagement. Now, if you look at video engagement as a standard set of reports, so the reports here up until the ones with the X marks on the right, these are all standard Facebook reports. So if I look at video engagement as a standard report from Facebook, there's some useful information. So I can see for this particular period, how many impressions I've had, two second video replays, three second, 10 seconds, through plays, et cetera, et cetera. Except I can't really see much more about, for example, am I getting sales from my video campaigns and a few other metrics? So what I do in this instance is I go to customize columns, I reorder them based on what I'm actually looking for, and then I save it as a custom report. So just to kind of show you that process, is you're going to, you know, you go into a report that has some data that you want, you can go in and just reorder, add items in, and just customize it for your own purposes. So for example, here, you know, I might, for example, take out the two second, I'm not so interested in that, maybe the three second, for example, add a few things in, so I might go into standard events, add to car, et cetera. And then what I would do is, as you can see here, as a pre-saved audience, is I've created the column and ordering that makes most sense to me. So I've started off with spend, and then I've added delivery, so are they active, what's the status, et cetera. Impressions, reach, CPM, which is something I'm interested in, 10 second views, et cetera, et cetera, through plays, and we can go all the way down, we can look at our video funnel, people that view 25% of the video, 50, et cetera, et cetera, all the way through, and I'm looking at ROAS here, just to see what kind of happens with video ads as well. Now the only thing about this is, I'm looking at video engagement metrics, but what's being run in these ad sets is a mixture of video or non-video. So what you can actually do is to do a search for the objectives, and you can add video. Now what I'm doing is I'm specifically looking for certain videos that I've set up under the video view engagement objective. What I wanna do here is these videos have been created specifically to create new audiences at low cost. So if you're thinking about, you might be in a niche that's quite competitive. So I'll give you an example. I operate in the niche around info products.

## [03:01 - 06:02]

So I sell training programs, coaching programs, et cetera, it's a very expensive niche. You could be paying, I don't know, 40, 60, $80 CPM. So my costs are really high in that niche. However, if I create video campaigns at top of funnel, I can get far lower CPMs like I do on my ad account, and as we are on this e-com account. So on average, we spent 3K to reach nearly, over 1 million people and generate 1.7 million impressions at a cost of $1.74 CPM. That means for $1.74, just under $2, I can reach 1,000 people with my video. That's great, but I wanna see how well that money has been spent. And then I would look at my cost per 10 second video view. So how much is it costing me for someone to watch 10 seconds? But the actual key metric I look at is through play. So through play is continuous viewing. 0.01 is okay, it's not actually that groundbreaking. I have ad accounts running that have 0.0001 dollar per action for through play because they're reaching many people and lots of people are engaging. So even though this is a good cost, so to reach 1,000 people and get them engaging in my videos is not costing me a lot at all, but I know that I can do better. So then I'm kinda looking down at the funnel and seeing kinda what else is going on. And I can see, for example, people are purchasing straight off the video. So the videos aren't there for me to generate sales. They're there to create remarketing audiences. So for this one, we created a super look-alike audience, SLAA, a 10%, which is a big look-alike. So super look-alike is a multiple look-alike. So it might be a look-alike of add to car, purchase, high value customer. So that for me is a super look-alike. And we've grown 10%, so big as possible. And in this dynamic content optimized ad, we're sharing out about us video. So for this brand, we've gone deep into sharing the values and who this brand are, what they stand for, et cetera. Not massive spends right now, but what we're trying to do is optimize the ad to get us the lowest cost per through play. So this is okay, but we know we can do so much better as well. This is how we broaden the top of funnel for our ad campaigns. Doesn't matter if it's E-com, info product, whatever the niche is, this is a great way of reaching lots of people on Facebook, as long as you have good quality video content. And then you can remarket to people that have watched a certain percentages. So for the next part of this funnel, we test targeting those that viewed 25%, those that viewed 50%, 75%, 95%, 100, to gauge where the quality is.

## [06:02 - 07:43]

So you create a remarketing audience based on this video, based on them reaching different parts, and then we'd run that as a test and see which of these audiences perform best with our highest performing ads. And that audience is the one that we funnel everyone into. So it might be, for example, I think for this one it's 50% plus. So anyone that's viewed 50% or more of the video gets hit with a direct response ad. And the cool thing about that is then we can create another lookalike audience. So people that have viewed 50%, we create another lookalike, go 10%, and create even more highly targeted, highly engaged people at really, really low cost. And it's a great way of audience building so that you can actually build people down your funnel, give them an experience. So this product, for example, the average order values are at about $100, and we are trying to create a category leader with this Ecom brand. And by that, what I mean is we wanna be seen as the go-to place for this particular style of apparel. So we don't wanna just go top of funnel with direct response and say, here's a great product, buy it, maybe there's a discount, whatever, and talk quite heavily about the product. We want to bring people in on the brand. The founder has a fascinating story. The founder's a big part of this business as well. And we wanna share that to people because we know that the brand has a big impact. The founder's story can have a big impact on the people they're trying to help. And we're bringing them through a video as well. So that's just some insight into how I would set up the columns, but how I would use something like video engagement as well.
